source_text: SAMUEL, EDWIN HERBERT, B.A. Oxon (Balliol Coll.). —B. 1898; ed. Westminster; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1917; G. S. I., G. H. Q., Palestine, 1917-19; dist. offr., J'lem dist., Palestine civil admtn., Oct., 1920; dist. offr., Ramallah sub.-dist., 1925; dist. offr., Jaffa, 1926; sec., wages comsn., 1927; asst. sec., Pal. govt., 1927; ag. priv. sec. to O. A. G., 1929; dist. offr., J'lem dist., 1930; Commonwealth fund serv. fellowship (econ.), Columbia Univ., New York, 1931-2; ag. asst. dist. comsr., J'lem dist., 1932; mem., rev. coll. comsn., 1933; asst. dist. comsr., Galilee divn., 1933-4; dep. comsr., migrn., 1934.

SAVAGE, A. W., C.M.G. (1954).—b. 1898; ed. Northern Poly., Northampton Inst., Sheffield Univ., and Grahame White Flying Sch.; mil. serv., 1916–19; aer. inspn. directorate, air min., 1921; secon., Indian govt., 1934–35, Egyptian govt., 1936–45 and C.O., 1946; dir., civ. aviation, W.A., Nig., 1946; Mal., 1949; dir.-gen., Mal./Borneo region, 1953.

SIMS, GEORGE, M.A. (Oxon.), B. Eden.—B. 1908; ed. Chesterfield Grammar non-collegiate soc., Oxford; asst. master, dept., Hong Kong, 1930; lect., tech. inst., 35; govt. exam., colloquial Cantonese, headmaster, govt. secondary schl., Cyprus, 1936. SIMS, JAMES HUGH, C.B.E. (1936).—B. 1889; sub.-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Police, inspr., 1913; asst. comsnr., 1918; dep. comsnr. and govt. sec., 1934.

STEYN, DANIEL HENDRIK, Dr. Econ. (Zürich, Switzerl.)—B. 1901; ed. Gymnasium Potchefstroom, Stellenbosch Univ., Com. Coll., Vienna, Zürich Univ.; tech. asst., dept. agr., Jan., 1928; economist, Board of Trade and Industries, Union of S. Africa, June, 1929; mem., Board of Trade and Industries, July, 1934; econ. advr., do., Jan., 1937; profi. asst., dept. of finance and econ. advr., dept. of commerce and industries, May, 1939.

STURGES, ROBERT SIMON MANSFIELD, O.B.E. (29).—B. 1893; ed. Radley and Christ Ch.; st. pol. offr., Iraq civ. admin., Apr., 1920; ternal oriental sec. to high commr., Iraq, Feb., 21; asst. sec., Apr., 1921; polit. sec., Oct., 24. *STURROCK, SIR JOHN CHRISTIAN RAMSAY, t. Bach. (1934), C.M.G. (1927), M.A. (Oxon).— 1875; ed. Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., xford; tutor to H.H. the Kabaka of Buganda, ar., 1905; dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., Aug., 14; prov. comsnr., June, 1921; ag. ch. sec., one, 1924; res. comsnr., Basutoland, 1926; ret., 35.

SUTHERLAND, HON. DONALD MATHESON, P.C. (Can.), M.B., D.S.O.—B. 1879; 1st el. to rep. S. Oxford in Ont. legis., g. e., May, 1902; re-el., g. e., Jan., 1905; dir., colonization and immigrn., Ontario, Mar. 1909; el. at g. e., Dec., 1921; re-elect., g.e., 1925 and 1930; min. of national defence in Bennett cabinet, Aug., 1930; re-elect. by accl. after assuming office, Aug. 1930.

THOMSON, William Patrick O'Brien.—b. 1894; ed. at Bristol; copyist., registr. of titles off., J'ca., 1910; cl. asst., col. sec., 1911; asst., 1913; 2nd. cl. clk., 1920; 1st cl., 1925; prin., 1936; registr.-gen. and dep. keeper of records, 1942.

TINWORTH, WILLIAM LEONARD, A.M.Inst.T. — B. 1885; mil. serv., India and Mesopotamia, 1917-19; asst. acctnt., F.M.S. rlys., 1921; ag. dep. acctnt., 1926, 1929 and 1930; ag. acctnt., pub. wks. dept., 1929; dep. ch. acctnt., Tanganyika rlys., 1931; ag. ch. acctnt., 1933; ch. acctnt., 1936; ag. gen. man., 1938.

THOMSON, Walter Morris.—b. 1905; ed. Royal High Sch. and Edin. Univ., M.A. (hons.); on mil. serv. 1944-46, col.; cadet, H.K., 1928; asst. sec., Chinese affrs., 1931; 3rd. asst. col. sec., 1935; senr. asst. col. treas., 1935; asst. supt., impts. and expts. dept., 1937; ch. W.I. section, Br.Col. Missn., Washington, 1942; dep. ch. planner, H.K., C.O., 1944; dep. ch. civ. affrs. offr., H.K., 1945; dir., supplies, trade and indus. and supt., impts. and expts., 1946; dep. dir., dept. of comm. and indus., Nig., 1948 (secondment); ch. advsr., c.-in-c., H.K., at 1st F.E. Conf., Singapore, 1946; main deleg. to negotiate revd. cust. and terr. agreemt, China and H.K., 1946; mem., tax and inc. tax comsn., H.K., 1947; mem., comsn. on future of port of H.K., 1947; econ. advsr., Br. del. at U.N.E.C. for rehab. of Asia, and the F.E., 1947.

THOMAS, MAJOR KENNETH ROBERT.—B. 1880; ed. Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; atty.-gen.'s office, Cape Town, 1897; relieving offr., dept. of just., 1899; ag. mag., Taungs, Barkly West, Mateking, Somerset East, Knysna, Piquetberg, Victoria West, Jansenville, Somerset West, Wynberg, Vryburg, Carnarvon, Aliwal North, Naauwport, Griquatown, George, Cape Town, Indwe, Queenstown, Sterkstroom, Glen Grey, Worcester, Tulbagh, Uitenhage, Britstown, Victoria East, Okahandja, Windhoek, Walvis Bay, Swakopmund, Karibib, Germiston, Boksburg, Rustenburg, Pretoria, Johannesburg, East London; mily. serv., Boer War, 1899-1902 (siege, Kimberley), Great War, 1914-18; mily. duty, S.W. Africa, 1915-25; mily. mag., Okahandja; mag., Durban, 1938.

TURBET, Charles Rupert, R.M.C.V.S. B.V.Sc., F.R.S.T.M. & H.— b. 1897; ed. Sydney Univ. and R.V. Coll., Lond.; on mil. serv., 1915–19 and 1941–44, maj.; gov. vet. offr., Fiji, 1923; senr., 1929; off. M.L.C., 1937–38; specialist in animal husbandry, Ceylon (secondment), 1945; resumed duty, Fiji, 1948; senr. animal husbandry offr., Nyasa., 1949; chmn., rice growing and milling industry comtee., Fiji, 1938; rep. Fiji, world's poultry congress, U.S.A., 1939; chmn., serv. rehab. comtee, Fiji, 1943–44; mission to B. Sol. Is. Prot. re. agric. and livestock rehab. and dev.; mem., bd. of trustees, Fiji museum, 1936; off. mem., Suva Town Bd. 1940–44; co-founder, Fiji soc. of sci. and indus. (now Fiji soc.), 1938; author of numerous articles and reports on animal husbandry, pastures and vet. sci., report on Br. Sol. Is. Prot. mission, and Development of Cattle Farms in Ceylon , 1948.

VICKERS, W. J., C.M.G. (1950), C.St.J. (1951).—b. 1898; ed. privately, Birm. Med. Sch. and Camb. Univ.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; mil. serv., 1917-19, 1945-46, col.; res. med. staff, gen. hosp., Birm., 1922; F.M.S. med. serv., 1925; S.M.O., Pal., 1938; secon. nutritional advr., D.W.W.I., 1944-45; re-apptd. Mal. med. serv., 1945; D.M.S., S'pore, 1946; mem. advsy. coun., S'pore, 1946-48; M.L.C.; A nutritional economic survey of war-time Palestine , 1942-43, and several anti-malaria surveys.

WACHER, LAWRENCE FORD, M.B.E. (1937).—B. 1884; ed. Waldon House Schl., Herne Bay, Kent Coast Coll. and Agrl. Coll., Wye; mem., staff, Wye Coll., 1910; agrl. offr., Basutoland, 1911; war serv., 1915-19; 2nd lieut., H.C.H.B., 1915; lieut., 1916; ag. capt. and adjt. 2nd D.A.C., 1918; returned to Basutoland, 1920.

WAKEFIELD, BRIG.-GEN. HUBERT STEPHEN, O.B.E (Mil.) 1919—B. 1883; ed. private. schl. and Ellesmere Coll.; joined Cape Mtd. Riflemen, 1901; S.A. war, 1899-02; (Queen's med., 3 clasps); lieut., C.M R., 1912; lieut., 1st regt., S.A.M.R., 1913; operns. in G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15; adjt., 1st Regt., S.A.M.R., 1915-16; capt., 1st S.A. Infy. (Overseas), 1917; operns., France and Belgium, 1917-18; capt., 1st Res. Batt., S.A. Infy. and seconded for sp. employment, 1918; D.A.Q.M.G. and temp. major, 1918; operns., Persia, 1918-19; ment. in desps., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory medals; on staff of O.C., Union garrison troops, Cape Peninsula, with temp. rank of capt., 1921; D.A.A.G. (later staff offr.) defence hdqrs., Pretoria, 1921; capt., 1922; posted S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; maj., do., 1929; O.C., No. 1 mil. dist., Cape Town, with temp. rank of lt.-col., 1931; lt.-col., S.A. Staff Corps, 1931; O.C. No. 4 mil. dist., Johannesburg, 1932; adjt.-gen., U.D.F., 1934; temp. col., 1934; col., 1935; brig.-gen., Sept., 1937; dep. ch., gen. staff, retaining appt. of adjt.-gen., Sept., 1937.
WORKMAN, Col. John Edward.— b. 1897 ; ed. privately ; asst. comsnr., police, G.C., 1925 ; comsnr., police, Fiji, 1939 ; comndt., Fiji Defence Force, col., 1939 ; comsnr., police, N. Rhod., 1946.

ADEMOLA, Sir A. A., Kt. (1957).—b. 1906; ed. King's Coll., Lagos, and Selwyn Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple; mag., Nig., 1939; puisne judge, 1949; chief justice, W. regn., 1955; chief justice, fed. sup. ct., 1958; mem. of comsns. for revision of cts. legn. (1948) and to enq. into Enugu disturbances (1949), Nig.

ALEXANDER, W. G., M.B.E.—b. 1918 ; ed. Sedbergh Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford ; mil. serv., 1940–45, major ; admin. cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946 ; co-op. soc. offr., 1947 ; registr., co-op. soc., Maur., 1951 ; comsnr., co-op. devel., Cyp., 1955 ; U.K. delegate, F.A.O./I.L.O. tech. mtg. on co-ops. in Asia and F.E., 1954 ; survey and Report on possibilities co-op. dev. and necessary co-op. legislation in G. & E. Is., 1947.

BACON, R. S., M.B.E. (1943).—b. 1895 ; ed. Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford ; called to bar, Middle Temple ; mil. serv., 1914-19 and (staff list) 1940-46, col. ; pvte. prac., London, 1923-39 ; ch. just., Gib., 1946 ; justice of appeal, E.A. ct., 1955 ; carried out police enquiry, Gib., 1946 ; author of Commercial Arbitrations .

BECKWITH, P. W., O.B.E. (1964).—b. 1917; ed. Jamaica Coll. and Wolmer's Sch., J'ca; senr. clk. J'ca, 1948; prin., 1950; admin. sec., 1950; asst. sec., 1953; secon. Leeward Is. as econ. and fin. advr., 1952; prin. asst. sec. J'ca. 1955; under sec., min. of fin., 1958. (J'ca Govt. service.)

BERNACCHI, M. L., Lt.-Cmdr., R.N. (Retd.), C.M.G. (1955), O.B.E. (1952).—b. 1911 ; ed. Dartmouth and Greenwich R.N.Cs., and Magdalene Coll., Camb. ; Royal Navy, 1925–34 ; mil. serv., 1940–44, R.N. ; cadet, Fiji, 1936 ; dist. comsnr., 1937 ; trans. Mal., spec. duty, N. Borneo, 1944 ; mil. admin., 1945–46, colonel ; Mal., 1947 ; cl. IC., M.C.S., 1951 ; Perak Meritorious Service Medal, 1951 ; res. comsnr., G. & E. Is. Col., 1952.
BRUNDELL, J. E.—b. 1914 ; ed. Auckland Gram. Sch. and Auckland Univ. Coll. ; nav. serv., 1942–46 ; cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1942 ; admin. offr., gd II, 1945.

BRYANT, Herbert Collingwood Algernon.—b. 1908; ed. Bishop's Stortford Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Oxon); cadet, Nig., 1931; asst. dist. offr., Nig., 1934; dist. offr., 1941; dist. comsnr., G. and E. Is. Col., 1946; 1st asst. sec., W. Pac. H.C., 1948; ag. High Comsnr., July–Aug., 1950.

BROWNLEES, J. K.—b. 1910; ed. Latymer Sch., Edmonton, and Keble Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); apptd. B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1933; secon. Tonga as sec. to govt., 1941–48; trans. to Nig., 1948; land offr., W. regn., 1952; comsnr., lands, 1954; ret., re-app. jud. comsnr., legal advr. and Br. judge of jt. court, N. Heb., 1956–58; chmn., comsn. of enq. into Tongan pub. serv., 1946.

BRYANT, H. C. A., O.B.E. (1953).—b. 1908; ed. Bishop's Stortford Coll. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; cadet, Nig., 1931; asst. dist. offr., Nig., 1934; dist. offr., 1941; admin. offr., cl. I, G. & E. Is. Col., 1946; 1st asst. sec., W. Pac. H.C., 1948; ag. high comsnsr., periods, 1950 and 1952; admin. offr., gr. II, Nig., 1953. (Nig. Govt. service.)

CAMBRIDGE, T. C., O.B.E. (1963).—b. 1906; ed. Queen's Royal Coll.; mil. serv., 1939–40, lieut.; clk., Trin., 1924; asst. warden, dist. admin., 1936; warden, 1944; senr., 1956; comsnr., local govt., 1958; perm. sec., min. of works, 1959. (T'dad Govt. service.)

CHAPMAN, Harold Arthur Waterlow.—b. 1900 ; ed. Marlborough Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford, M.A. (Oxon), final hon. sch. lit. hum., class. exhibr. ; asst. mstr., Watford Gram. Sch. ; asst. mstr., Harrison Coll., Barb., 1927–30 ; prin., educ. dept., Ken., 1930 ; prin., gov. African sec. sch., 1947.

CLEMENS, Warren Frederick Martin, M.C. (1942).—b. 1915 ; ed. Bedford Sch. and Christ's Coll., Camb., B.A. (Cantab.) ; on mil. serv., 1942–44, maj. ; cadet, col. serv., 1938 ; dist. comsnr., B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1944 ; asst. dist. comsnr., Pal., 1946 ; admin. offr., Cyp., 1948.

COOPER, H. R. R. L.—b. 1925; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, C.I. and Lancing Coll., Dublin; mil. serv., 1944–47, lieut.; cadet, G. & E. Is. Col., 1952; admin. offr., 1955; dep. registr. co-op. socs., Fiji, 1962.
FITZWILLIAM, N. B. G.—b. 1922 ; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin. ; temp. solr., cr. solr's dept., Trin., 1947 ; asst. cr. solr., 1949 ; registr. and marshal, sup. ct., 1951.

FEURTADO, Wesley Yorke, A.M.Inst. C.E., L.R.I.B.A., M.R.San.I.—b. 1900 ; ed. Munro Coll., Jca. ; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jca., 1927 ; ch. draughtsman, 1934 ; D.D.P.W., 1941 ; D.P.W., 1947 ; mem. and chmn. of various comtees. and bds.

FOSTER-SUTTON, Sir Stafford, K.B.E. (1957), Kt. (1951), C.M.G. (1958), O.B.E. (Mil.), (1944), Q.C. (J'ca) (1938), (F. of Mal.), (1948).—b. 1897 ; ed. St. Mary Magdalen Sch. and private tutor ; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn ; mil. serv., 1914–26 ; solr.-gen., J'ca, 1936 ; atty.-gen., Cyp., 1940 ; mem. for law and order and atty.-gen., Ken., 1944 ; ag. gov., 1947 ; atty.-gen., Mal., 1948 ; o.a.g., 1950 ; ch. just., F. of Mal., 1950 ; pres., W.A. ct. of appeal, 1951 ; ch. just., fed. sup. ct., Nig., 1955–58 ; colonel cmdg. Cyp. vol. force and inspr., Cyp. forces, 1941–44 ; dir., man-power, Ken., 1944–45 ; chmn., labr. advsy. bd. ; European civil serv. advsry. bd., Ken., 1944–48.

GERMOND, J. D. A., C.B.E., M.B.E. (mil.)—b. 1904 ; mil. serv., 1941–45, major ; clk., Bech. Prot., 1925 ; asst. dist. comsnr., 1936 ; admin. offr., gr. I, B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1947–50 ; dist. offr., Bech. Prot., 1950 ; admin. offr., staff gr., 1951 ; divisional comsnr., 1954.

HOPE-JONES, Arthur.—b. 1911; ed. Cambridge Univ., Columbia Univ., Fellow Christ Coll., Cambridge, M.A. (Cantab.); on mil. serv. 1939–46, ag. capt.; economic and commercial advsr., Ken. govt., 1946; author of numerous articles in journals of public opinion and learned societies, also Income Tax in England during the Napoleonic Wars , 1939; mem., Ken. dev. comtee. and trades disputes tribunal.

INCE, R. E.—b. 1905 ; ed. Brentwood Sch. and Camb. Univ. ; p.o.w., 1942–45 ; mstr., King Edward VII Sch., Taiping, 1927 ; asst. inspr. of schs., Penang, 1936 ; headmstr., govt. sch., 1937 ; senr. inspr., schs., Mal., 1946 ; S'pore, 1951 ; dep. dir., educ., 1952 ; dep. sec., min. of educ., 1955.

JOHN, I. G.—b. 1912 ; ed. Gowerton Gram. Sch. and Univ. of Wales ; mil. serv., 1941–46 ; prob. met. offr., S.S., 1937 ; met. offr., 1939 ; S'pore, 1948 ; asst. dir., Mal. met. servs., 1951 ; dir., 1953.

LACY, Donald Percival.—b. 1902; ed. Sec. Sch.; asst. clk., P.W.D., J'ca., 1918; 2nd cl., 1923; 1st cl., lands dept., 1936; fin. clk., P.W.D., 1942; prin. clk., 1943; asst. man. and acctnt., gov. savings bk., 1948; man., 1950.

LEECHMAN, B., C.M.G. (1952), O.B.E. (1941).—b. 1901 ; ed. Oundle Sch. ; cadet, Tang., 1925 ; asst. dist. offr., 1928 ; dist. offr., 1937 ; dep. prov. comsnr., 1944 ; labr. comsnr., 1946 ; mem. for labr., educ. and soc. welfare, 1948 ; mem. for soc. serv., 1948 ; sec., E.A. econ. coun., Nairobi, 1940–41 ; dir., econ. cont., Aden, 1943–45 ; sec., maj. wks. comtee., 1937 ; sec., cent. dev. comtee., 1939–40 ; dep. food and export contlr., 1939–40 ; sec., E.A. del., E. group confce., 1940 ; E.A. del. to U.S.A. and Rhod. ; war prod. and supply liaison, 1941 ; mem. econ. cont. bd., 1942–43 ; i/c. rubber prod., 1942–43 ; dep. chmn. and exec. offr., econ. cont. bd., 1945–46.

LONGSWORTH, Alfred Owen, M.B.E.—b. 1891; ed. C. of E. Coll., Belize; clk., B. Hond., 1909; 3rd cl. clk., 1912; 2nd cl., 1918; 1st cl., 1923; clk. to registr.-gen., 1933; registr.-gen., 1938; cust. of enemy prop. and census offr.

MACINTYRE, W., L.—b. 1915; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Trin.; solr., Trin.; barrister-at-law; registr. and addl. mag., St. Kitts, 1946; mag., 1st dist., St. L., 1950; mag., Grenada, 1952; cr. atty., St. L., 1960; atty.-gen., Antigua, 1963.

MAYERS, T. H.. Q.C.—b. 1907 ; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb. and Downing Coll., Camb. ; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple ; res. mag., J'ca, 1936 ; solr.-gen., 1940 ; atty.-gen., 1943 ; puisne judge, Ken., 1952 ; M.E.C. and M.L.C. ; mem. priv. coun. ; chmn., comsn. of enq. into leakage of offl. infn., 1945 ; closer assoc. conf., Montego Bay, 1947 ; chmn., J'ca govt. sugar del. to U.K., 1949.

MULLIGAN, H. W., C.M.G. (1954).—b. 1901 ; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen and Chicago Univs. ; mil. serv., 1940–43, col. ; I.M.S., 1923 ; dir., W.A. inst. for trypan. research, 1947 ; lecturer, L/N., 1936–37 ; health surv. and dev. comtee., India, 1943–45 ; numerous papers on protozoology, immunology, pathology, etc.

PULFREY, W., O.B.E. (1960).—b. 1908; ed. Central Sch. and Univ., Sheffield; asst. geologist, Ken., 1935; senr., 1945; chief geol., 1949; comsnr., mines and geol., 1959; sec., dev. sub-comtee. on bldg. materials, 1945; author of numerous articles on geol. matters.

RAMSAY, N. J. G.—b. 1916; ed. Merchiston Castle Sch. and Edin. Univ.; nav. serv., 1940–46, lt. ; adminr.-gen., N. Rhod., 1947; res. mag., 1955/6; senr. resdt. mag., 1958; puisne judge, 1964. (Zambia Govt. service.)
RICHARDS, H. G., M.B.E. (1961).—b. 1916; ed. Primary Sch., St. Helena; clk., St. Helena, 1933; ch. clk., 1949; office supt., 1953; asst. govt. sec., 1962.

SCOTT, Mrs. G. L. N.—b. 1923; ed. Hampton Sch., and Lond. Sch. of Econ.; senr. housing offr., J'ca., 1948; Carib. comsn., 1949; statistician, 1952; asst. sec., cent. plann. unit, 1956; prin. asst. sec., 1961.

SHIPP, Guy William Ironside.—b. 1907 ; ed. Charterhouse, exhibnr. and R.M.C., Sandhurst, 2nd lieut., 1927 ; Oxford Univ. post-grad. col. serv. course, 1935–36 (1st cl.) ; on mil. serv., 1939–45, col. ; cadet, T.T., 1936 ; prin. civil dispersal offr., 1945 ; seconded E.A.H.C., 1948.

SHIELDS, J. V. M., C.B.E. (1957), O.B.E. (1946), Q.C.—b. 1914 ; ed. Dardenne Sch. and Univ. of Glasgow ; admitted Faculty of Advocates, Scotland ; mil. serv., 1939–46, col. ; exec. sec., Br. sectn., Caribbean comsn., 1946 ; fed. coun., Mal., 1947 ; ag. senr., fed. coun., 1949 and 1951 ; atty.-gen., Aden, 1952 ; rev. comsnr., laws of Aden, 1955.

SHIPP, G. W. I.—b. 1907; ed. Charterhouse, R.M.C., Sandhurst, and Oxford Univ.; Army, 1927-35; mil. serv., 1939-45, col.; cadet, T.T., 1936; asst. dist. offr., 1938; prin. civil dispersal offr., 1945-48; dist. offr., 1948; sec., E.A.H.C. (secon.), 1948; dist. comsnr., Aden, 1951; M.E.C., 1951.

SKERL, Anton Francis, A.R.S.M., B.Sc. (eng.) (min.) (Lond.), A.R.I.C.S., A.M.I.M.M.—b. 1905 ; ed. Westminster City Sch., Royal Sch. of Mines and Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Tech. ; inspr. mines, T.T., 1936 ; senr. inspr., 1945 ; part author of bulletin Mica in Tanganyika Territory .

SINCLAIR, P. L. G.—b. 1910 ; ed. St. Felix Coll., Suva ; p.o.w., 1941–42 ; mil. serv., 1942–45, R.N.Z.N., ch. E.R.A. ; relieving engnr., H.M.C.S. Nimanoa , 1936–47 ; engnr., 1937 ; mech. engnr., G. & E. Is. Col., 1951.

TOURS, K. C., C.M.G. (1955).—b. 1908 ; ed. Aldenham Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. ; mil. serv., 1945-46, col. (B.M.A.) ; cadet, G.C., 1931 ; asst. dist. comsnr., 1932 ; Gam., 1935 ; spec. duty, sec't., 1936 ; asst. col. sec., 1937 ; asst. dist. comsnr., Pal., 1938 ; dep. food contrlr., 1941 ; M.P.U., Mal., 1945 ; food contrlr., 1945 ; chmn., jt. supply bd., 1946 ; prin. asst. sec., sec't., 1948 ; dep. fin. sec., 1949 ; perm. sec., fin., G.C., 1950 ; fin. sec. and min., fin., 1954 ; econ. advr., 1954 (Ghana civil service).

WADDELL, Sir Alexander, K.C.M.G. (1959), C.M.G. (1955), D.S.C. (1944).—b. 1913; ed. Fettes Coll. and Edin. Univ.; nav. serv., 1942–44, lieut.; mil. serv., 1944–47, lt. col.; W.O., 1944; cadet, B. Sol. Is. Prot., 1937; dist. offr., 1938; dist. comsnr., 1945; o.a.g., 1945; M.C.S., 1945; admin. offr., N. Borneo, 1946; prin. asst. sec., 1947; col. sec., Gam., 1952; chief sec., S.L., 1956; dep. gov., 1958; gov. and c.-in-c., S'wak, 1960–63.

WILES, D. A., C.M.G. (1965), O.B.E. (1960).—b. 1912; ed. Harrison Coll., Barb., and Univs. of Lond. and Toronto; on staff Harrison Coll., 1935; public librarian, Barb., 1945; asst. col. sec., 1950; perm. sec., 1954; adminr., Montserrat, 1960–64.

WEDDERBURN, C.M.G. (1935).—B. 18 Coll., Edinburgh, and cadet, Ceylon civ. serv. Point Pedro and Chas Matara, Aug., 1909; K. land settlmt. offr., Jan. offr. and special off. Ordce.," Aug., 1912 asst. land settlmt. o "Waste Lands O ensor, Nov., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Liya, Sept., 1915; asst. settlmt. offr., July, 1920; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; govt. ag., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1925; addnl. govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1928; govt. agt., S. Prov., Apr., 1929; ls. I, grade I, 1931; govt., agt., W. Prov., Jan., 1931; ag. ch. sec., Dec., 1932; offr. apptd. to report on poor relief, Aug., 1933; dep. ch. sec., Sept., 1934; ag. ch. sec., Oct. 1934-Mar., 1935 and June-Nov., 1935; ch. sec., 1936.

BEVERLEY, WM. HENRY. — Ed. Bishop's high schl., Poona, India; F.R.G.S.; 2nd lieut., 4th Norfolk regt., 1889; lieut., 3rd Essex regt., 1896; capt., ditto, 1899; sub-commdt., R. Niger Co.'s constab., and govr., Asaba Gaol, 1899; Wing offr., N.C. Prot. Force (afterwards S. Nigeria regt.), 1900; intell. offr., S. Nig. regt., 1903; operations in Niger Co.'s territories, 1899 (medal and clasp); Obium expedn., 1901; Oran expedn., 1901; Aro expedn. (medal and clasp), 1901; Asaba hinterland, 1902; Uri Omonoha hinterland, 1903.

D'AETH, JOHN.—Ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jamaica, Jan., 1873; ch. ditto, Mar., 1875; dist. engnr., N. (now W.) Dist., Jan., 1883; engnr., Kingston Dist., 1886; asst. dir. pub. wks., Jamaica, 10th Mar., 1902.

DANSEY, CLAUDE EDWARD MARJORIBANKS. —Ed. at Wellington; late lieut., Lancas. Fusiliers; capt., reserve of officers; served during Matabeleland rebellion, 1896, with Matabele mounted pol. (medal); sub. comdt., B. N. Borneo constaby. during operations against rebels under Mohamed Salleh, May, 1899, to Jan., 1900 (medal); commanded squadron 1st African Light Horse during South African War, 1900-2 (2 medals and 6 clasps); staff-lieut. for intell. and A.D.C. to G.O.C., Harrismith dist., S. Africa, 1902-4; specially mentd. by gov. of B. N. Borneo for services during expedn. in interior. and mentd. in desp., S. Africa, 1900; asst. dist. offr., Somaliland, 4th Nov., 1904; 2nd asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Aug., 1905.

flagged_value.position: sub. comdt., B. N. Borneo constaby.
staff-lieut. for intell. and A.D.C. to G.O.C., Harrismith district

source_text: D'AETH, JOHN, I.S.O. (1910), M.I.C.E.—Ed. at King's Coll., Lond.; asst. draughtsman, P.W.D., Jamaica, Jan., 1873; ch. ditto, Mar., 1875; dist. engnr., W. dist., Jan., 1883; engnr., Kingston dist., Aug., 1886; S. dist., Apr., 1892; Kingston and E. dist., Apr., 1899; seconded for service as col. engnr. and surv.-gen., Dominica, Jan., 1901; asst. D.P.W., Jamaica, Mar., 1902; ag. D.P.W., 1902, 1903, 1905 and 1907.

DE ROS, COLIN JOSEPH FERNANDEZ STANISLAUS. — Ed. at Catholic gram. sch., Georgetown, B. Guiana; served as a volunteer customs, 1st Apr. to 31st Dec., 1885; 6th cls. of 1st Jan., 1886; 5th cls. offr., 16th Dec., 1890; 4th cls. offr., 1st Sept., 1892; 3rd cls. offr., 4th June, 1896; 2nd cls. offr., 14th Sept., 1905; 1st cls. offr., asst. to comptroller in excise matters, 1st Jan., 1906, to 31st Aug., 1902; ag. 1st cls. offr., 9th Aug., 1902.

DYER, THOS. THEODORE RODNEY.—B. 1869; entd. pub. ser. of St. Vincent as cust. offr., Jan., 1890; ag. clk., treasury, 1891; asst. clk., P.O., 2nd clk. govt. off. to Apr., 1892; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of govt., Windward Islds., May, 1892; transfd. to St. Lucia; 4th clk., treasury, May, 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1893; ag. 2nd clk., Feb. to May, 1893; 2nd clk., govt. off., May, 1893; also supt. of govt. printing; ag. 2nd clk. in off. of administr., Wind. Islds., 1894; hurricane relic offr., St. Vincent, Sept. to Dec., 1898; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of couns., St. Lucia, Oct., 1900, to Mar., 1901; apptd. ch. clk. to gov. Windward Is. (Grenada), 1st Apr., 1901; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. of ex. and leg. couns. 16th Nov., 1901; ag. pol. mag. N. dist.; asst. treas., Carriacou; chmn., road bd., local health authority and water authority, Carriacou, and pol. mag., dist. III., St. Vincent, Feb. to May, 1904; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of couns.; sec., hurricane loan bd.; keeper of telephone stores and med. registr., St. Vincent, May to Aug., 1904; offl. trustee, Grenada, for emigrts. who desire to deposit earnings in sav. bank.

FORESTIER-WALKER, GEN. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM EDWARD FORESTIER, G.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.B. (1894), C.B. (1878), C.M.G. (1886), Knight of Grace of St. John of Jerusalem.—B. 1844; ed. at Sandhurst; entd. Scots Guards, 1862; A.D.C., Mauritius, 1866-7; capt. and lieut.-col., 1873; mil. sec., Cape, 1873-8; col., 1878; maj.-gen., 1887; lieut.-gen., 1895; gen., 1902; served on staff in Kafir war, 1877-8; mil. sec. to Sir Bartle Frere, 1878-9; in Zulu war, 1879 (ment. in despa.); A.A. and Q.M.G., home district during war in Egypt, 1882; A.A. and Q.M.G., Bechuanaland, 1884; commdd. 2nd brig. at Aldershot, 1889-90; commdd. troops in Egypt, 1890-95; western dist., 1895-99; and troops in S. Africa, 1899; commdd. lines of communication in S. African war, 1899-1901; and troops in Cape Colony, 1900-1; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Gibraltar 1st Aug., 1905; also G.O.C. and high comsnr. in Mediterranean, 1909.

GROSS, D.—Apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and temporarily employed in the bd. of agric. and fisheries, Nov., 1906, to Apr., 1907; in the bd. of educn., Apl. to June, 1907; in the Scotch educn. dept., June to Aug., 1907; at general post office, Sept., 1907, to Jan., 1908; assigned to the C.O., Jan., 1908.
